beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and get ready for the day with coffee
beats.reasoning: Elena can't start the day without a swim, but it's early Monday — she'll do a quick morning routine at home first, then head to the pool. Full energy at start of simulation.

beats.activity: Commute to the pool for her morning swim
beats.reasoning: Elena's non-negotiable morning ritual — she needs her swim before work. Green Lake is a short drive from Capitol Hill and opens early on weekdays.
Elena starts every day in the water — this is her anchor ritual, especially on a sunny Monday when energy is high.
Elena heads to her workplace as an elementary school counselor. Light Rail is down between Westlake and U-District (9am–3pm), but she drives, so it doesn't affect her.

beats.activity: Work as elementary school counselor: morning sessions with students and staff
beats.reasoning: Elena is at work during the school day. The USA vs Wales FIFA match airs at 11am, but she's with students and won't watch — her work routine takes priority.
Elena grabs lunch in the school break room or nearby — short break before afternoon student sessions.

beats.activity: Work: afternoon counseling sessions and administrative tasks
beats.reasoning: Elena finishes her school day with afternoon student support and wrap-up tasks. School day ends around 3pm.

beats.activity: Drive to Capitol Hill thrift store for browsing
beats.reasoning: After work, Elena heads to her favorite hobby — thrifting. She values her routine and knows good spots in Capitol Hill. Sunny weather makes this a perfect Monday evening.

beats.activity: Browse thrift stores and hunt for vintage finds
beats.reasoning: Elena unwinds with her thrifting hobby on a gorgeous Monday. Sunny weather and good energy keep her out exploring. She's routine-bound and this is part of her regular rhythm.

beats.activity: Walk to dinner spot and grab a meal with a close friend
beats.reasoning: Elena values time with close friends over big crowds. She meets someone for dinner on the nice evening rather than watching the FIFA match at a crowded bar.
Elena enjoys an unhurried dinner catch-up with a close friend. This aligns with her preference for intimate social time and her routine Monday evening.

beats.activity: Walk home and wind down with a book and tea
beats.reasoning: Elena returns home for evening leisure — reading and relaxation before bed. She's already at home, so no commute needed before sleep.
Elena gets a full night of restorative sleep after an active day of swimming, work, thrifting, and socializing. Wakes at 6:30am for her next swim ritual.

diary:
Started the day the right way—early swim at Green Lake before work. The water was perfect, quiet before the city woke up. Got to Fremont Elementary with energy to spare, which I needed. Had back-to-back sessions with kids this morning, a couple of tough conversations with parents. Grabbed five minutes for lunch at my desk, honestly can't remember what I ate.

Afternoon was lighter, thank god. Around 1:30 I bailed early—the Light Rail was down anyway, so driving to Capitol Hill felt like the move. Spent a solid hour digging through thrift stores on Pine Street, found this gorgeous 70s ceramic bowl I didn't need but couldn't leave behind. Met up with Sarah for dinner as the sun was still high and warm. We talked about her new job, my exhausting week, the usual. Walked home slow, made tea, settled into my book. Felt like I could finally breathe.
thought_process: The World Cup match happening downtown didn't really pull me—I'm not much of a soccer person, though the bars being packed probably meant Capitol Hill would be busier later. The Light Rail closure actually worked in my favor; I'd been meaning to get to those thrift stores anyway, and driving was faster than waiting around. Work drained me more than usual today; a couple of the kids I'm seeing are going through real stuff, and it sits with me. I needed the afternoon reset—the thrift store browse and dinner with Sarah were less about the activities and more about decompressing. The weather helped. Sometimes a perfect 74-degree day and good company is exactly what you need to shake off the weight of other people's problems.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
